ops.title: iOS client (ios/CcPresent + CcPresentKit) shipped 2026-07-10 — what remains user-gated, plus local dev gotchas that live nowhere in the repo
ops.body:
The native SwiftUI iOS client landed on cc-present main 2026-07-10 (commits: daemon LAN surface, ios/ app + CcPresentKit, docs, ci-ios advisory-style fix), against cc-interact v0.6.0. Still user-gated afterward: TestFlight secrets (`ASC_KEY_ID`/`ASC_ISSUER_ID`/`ASC_KEY_P8`/`APPLE_TEAM_ID`) + App Store Connect app record for `com.yasyf.cc-present`, the one-time Xcode signing-team click, and the physical-iPhone pairing round trip.

Local gotchas not recorded in the repo:
- Daemon smoke tests under a temp HOME need a SHORT path (e.g. `/tmp/ccp-smoke`) — `~/.cc-present/daemon.sock` hits the macOS ~104-char `sun_path` limit and fails with `bind: invalid argument` under long scratchpad paths.
- This machine sets `GOFLAGS=-mod=mod` globally, which conflicts with `go.work` workspace mode; run `env GOFLAGS=-mod=readonly` when a workspace file is present.
- The `xcodebuildmcp` binary is wired in `.mcp.json` but NOT installed (`brew install xcodebuildmcp`); without it (or `idb`) simulator tap-through UI automation is impossible — smoke tests fall back to launch + screenshot + SSE-harness checks.
- The macos-26 CI runner's brew-latest swiftformat enables rules newer than local 0.62.1 — style steps are `continue-on-error: true` by policy (AGENTS.md: style never blocks).

**Why:** These four cost real debugging time during the iOS-client build-out and none are derivable from the repo.

**How to apply:** Recall when running daemon smokes, Go builds with a workspace, or simulator automation in cc-present. Related: [[cc-interact-release-shape]].
